source_jobs.title: General Application
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ATARAXIS AI

Ataraxis is a clinical AI research lab working at the intersection of multi-modal AI and precision medicine. Our goal is to make disease predictable. To accomplish this, we develop new AI methods that predict patient outcomes and treatment response, and build clinical tools to assist physicians in selecting the most optimal treatments for their patients.

Our AI research lab discovers and develops methods to recognize patterns and predict outcomes across complex, multi-modal clinical data. This spans our causality https://www.ataraxis.ai/research-causality (Ataraxis™ Tau), foundation model https://www.ataraxis.ai/research---representation-learning (Falcon and Kestrel for digital pathology), and survival analysis https://www.ataraxis.ai/research-survival-analysis research.

Our first clinical products, such as Ataraxis™ Breast https://www.ataraxis.ai/ataraxis-breast-overview for breast cancer, already help patients get the most appropriate treatment across the best academic institutions and community clinics worldwide.

At Ataraxis, you will have a unique opportunity to shape not only the future of our company, but also the future of healthcare. You will join an exceptional team at the forefront of clinical AI research and deployment. Our advisors include AI pioneers such as our founding advisor, Yann LeCun, and distinguished oncologists from top cancer research institutions, all united by the mission to redefine precision medicine.

Ataraxis has raised over $24 million in funding, including a $20 million Series A led by top venture capital funds such as Thiel Capital/Founders Fund (OpenAI, SpaceX, Palantir), Obvious Ventures (AMI Labs, Inceptive, Radical Numerics, Recursion), and AIX Ventures (Hugging Face, Perplexity).

We are an company with a flat organizational structure, where every team member is empowered to actively contribute. Leadership roles are earned by those who demonstrate initiative and consistently deliver exceptional results. Strong work ethic and the ability to prioritize ruthlessly are essential.




GENERAL APPLICATION

Please note that this general application is not monitored on a daily basis and you will hear from us only if there is a relevant opening that you might be a fit for, or if you have a very interesting background. We are always looking for talented people across all domain who want to contribute to our mission.







WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR

We are looking for people at any stage of their career who:

 1. Want to build a generational company with us, and want their work at Ataraxis to become a career-defining (or even life-defining) event.

 2. Are humble yet ambitious and confident. There is no work that's below you.

 3. Are self-motivated and relentless. You will have a lot of independence and freedom to act, and you are expected to capitalize on it.

 4. Have elite standards and consistently produce work at a level that sets the bar for others.

source_jobs.title: Founding Fellow, New Indications
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ATARAXIS AI

Ataraxis is a clinical AI research lab working at the intersection of multi-modal AI and precision medicine. Our goal is to make disease predictable. To accomplish this, we develop new AI methods that predict patient outcomes and treatment response, and build clinical tools to assist physicians in selecting the most optimal treatments for their patients.

Our AI research lab discovers and develops methods to recognize patterns and predict outcomes across complex, multi-modal clinical data. This spans our causality https://www.ataraxis.ai/research-causality (Ataraxis™ Tau), foundation model https://www.ataraxis.ai/research---representation-learning (Falcon and Kestrel for digital pathology), and survival analysis https://www.ataraxis.ai/research-survival-analysis research.

Our first clinical products, such as Ataraxis™ Breast https://www.ataraxis.ai/ataraxis-breast-overview for breast cancer, already help patients get the most appropriate treatment across the best academic institutions and community clinics worldwide.

At Ataraxis, you will have a unique opportunity to shape not only the future of our company, but also the future of healthcare. You will join an exceptional team at the forefront of clinical AI research and deployment. Our advisors include AI pioneers such as our founding advisor, Yann LeCun, and distinguished oncologists from top cancer research institutions, all united by the mission to redefine precision medicine.

Ataraxis has raised over $24 million in funding, including a $20 million Series A led by top venture capital funds such as Thiel Capital/Founders Fund (OpenAI, SpaceX, Palantir), Obvious Ventures (AMI Labs, Inceptive, Radical Numerics, Recursion), and AIX Ventures (Hugging Face, Perplexity).

We are an company with a flat organizational structure, where every team member is empowered to actively contribute. Leadership roles are earned by those who demonstrate initiative and consistently deliver exceptional results. Strong work ethic and the ability to prioritize ruthlessly are essential.


RESPONSIBILITIES

You will lead expansion of Ataraxis’s offerings to one of the next cancer types and disease areas beyond breast cancer. Ataraxis has already developed a pan-cancer AI platform that has been internally validated to perform well across various tumor types. This role will work on creating the right environment for the commercial launch in the next cancer type(s). In that process, you will:

 - Develop and manage a network of relationships with key opinion leaders, clinical advisors, early adopters, and research collaborators to identify potential customers and partners as well as validate hypotheses and gather insights

 - Develop internal knowledge (memos, draft collateral material) in preparation for medical affairs/sales teams in the future

 - Work directly with the CEO to execute on product launches in record speed

 - Source real-world datasets, clinical trial datasets, and biobank resources to support AI model development and evaluation

 - Partner with engineering and research teams to inform clinical-grade model development


QUALIFICATIONS

 - PhD, MD, or a Master’s degree in cancer biology, molecular biology, cell biology, biomedical sciences, pharmacology, pharmaceutical sciences, or a related life sciences field. You need to be able to very quickly learn about new disease areas in depth independently

 - Strong soft skills and being able to speak to various audiences, from medical key opinion leaders to software engineers. Ideally, you should have experience presenting scientific content to medical/academic audiences

 - Good sense/taste for product, creative thinking, being able to apply modern product building frameworks to healthcare

 - Bias for action, ability to form independent thought on complex topics where you don't have a lot of information and will have to trust your intuition, thinking from first principles

 - Strongly preferred: 2+ years of industry ex

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ATARAXIS AI

Ataraxis is a clinical AI research lab working at the intersection of multi-modal AI and precision medicine. Our goal is to make disease predictable. To accomplish this, we develop new AI methods that predict patient outcomes and treatment response, and build clinical tools to assist physicians in selecting the most optimal treatments for their patients.

Our AI research lab discovers and develops methods to recognize patterns and predict outcomes across complex, multi-modal clinical data. This spans our causality https://www.ataraxis.ai/research-causality (Ataraxis™ Tau), foundation model https://www.ataraxis.ai/research---representation-learning (Falcon and Kestrel for digital pathology), and survival analysis https://www.ataraxis.ai/research-survival-analysis research.

Our first clinical products, such as Ataraxis™ Breast https://www.ataraxis.ai/ataraxis-breast-overview for breast cancer, already help patients get the most appropriate treatment across the best academic institutions and community clinics worldwide.

At Ataraxis, you will have a unique opportunity to shape not only the future of our company, but also the future of healthcare. You will join an exceptional team at the forefront of clinical AI research and deployment. Our advisors include AI pioneers such as our founding advisor, Yann LeCun, and distinguished oncologists from top cancer research institutions, all united by the mission to redefine precision medicine.

Ataraxis has raised over $24 million in funding, including a $20 million Series A led by top venture capital funds such as Thiel Capital/Founders Fund (OpenAI, SpaceX, Palantir), Obvious Ventures (AMI Labs, Inceptive, Radical Numerics, Recursion), and AIX Ventures (Hugging Face, Perplexity).

We are an company with a flat organizational structure, where every team member is empowered to actively contribute. Leadership roles are earned by those who demonstrate initiative and consistently deliver exceptional results. Strong work ethic and the ability to prioritize ruthlessly are essential.


RESPONSIBILITIES

This is a high-ownership generalist role. You'll work across 3 services spanning TypeScript/React, Python backends, and ML/CV inference, all running on Google Cloud and Modal. Comfort moving between product code and ML infrastructure is essential.

 - Physician web portal: Next.js / React app where clinicians place orders, view slides, and read reports.

 - Order service: the system of record such as orders, patients, clinical data, insurance billing claims, report (PDF) generation, and a web of third-party integrations.

 - Slide tiles service: generates and serves DeepZoom pyramids from gigapixel pathology slides.


WHO YOU ARE

 - Product-obsessed builder: You're never satisfied with "good enough"—you relentlessly spot opportunities to improve the product and move fast to ship them, knowing that done and iterating beats perfect and waiting.

 - AI-native: You're comfortable using latest AI tools (e.g., Cursor/Windsurf) to quickly build, refine, and document your code.

 - Sense of style: You appreciate polished, professional UIs, enjoy collaborating closely with designers, and understand that great UX is integral to great products.

 - Creative thinker: You thrive in environments requiring innovation and aren't limited by past experience.

 - Self-directed leader: You take ownership, set your own agenda, and move with urgency. You don't need a roadmap handed to you. You identify what matters and rally others around it.

 - Effective collaborator: You keep teammates informed, surface blockers early, and turn around feedback rapidly, making it easy for those around you to do their best work.

 - Strong external communicator: You can represent the product and company confidently to customers, partners, and the outside world—translating technical work into clear, compelling narratives.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - Frontend
   
   - Strong TypeScript and React (we run React 19 /Next.js

source_jobs.title: Member of Technical Staff, Computational Pathology
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ATARAXIS AI

Ataraxis is a clinical AI research lab working at the intersection of multi-modal AI and precision medicine. Our goal is to make disease predictable. To accomplish this, we develop new AI methods that predict patient outcomes and treatment response, and build clinical tools to assist physicians in selecting the most optimal treatments for their patients.

Our AI research lab discovers and develops methods to recognize patterns and predict outcomes across complex, multi-modal clinical data. This spans our causality https://www.ataraxis.ai/research-causality (Ataraxis™ Tau), foundation model https://www.ataraxis.ai/research---representation-learning (Falcon and Kestrel for digital pathology), and survival analysis https://www.ataraxis.ai/research-survival-analysis research.

Our first clinical products, such as Ataraxis™ Breast https://www.ataraxis.ai/ataraxis-breast-overview for breast cancer, already help patients get the most appropriate treatment across the best academic institutions and community clinics worldwide.

At Ataraxis, you will have a unique opportunity to shape not only the future of our company, but also the future of healthcare. You will join an exceptional team at the forefront of clinical AI research and deployment. Our advisors include AI pioneers such as our founding advisor, Yann LeCun, and distinguished oncologists from top cancer research institutions, all united by the mission to redefine precision medicine.

Ataraxis has raised over $24 million in funding, including a $20 million Series A led by top venture capital funds such as Thiel Capital/Founders Fund (OpenAI, SpaceX, Palantir), Obvious Ventures (AMI Labs, Inceptive, Radical Numerics, Recursion), and AIX Ventures (Hugging Face, Perplexity).

We are an company with a flat organizational structure, where every team member is empowered to actively contribute. Leadership roles are earned by those who demonstrate initiative and consistently deliver exceptional results. Strong work ethic and the ability to prioritize ruthlessly are essential.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design and implement novel machine learning models and methods for computational pathology.

 - Translate machine learning and statistics papers into production-ready code.

 - Build robust model evaluation frameworks and monitor model performance.

 - Disseminate the results by co-authoring research papers and abstracts.

 - Collaborate with a multidisciplinary team of engineers and scientists.

 - Co-mentor junior members of the team.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - PhD degree in machine learning, computer vision or statistics.

 - Passion for research, attention to detail and ability to drive tasks to completion. Strong preference will be given to candidates with papers in A* conferences (e.g. ICML, ICLR, NeurIPS, CVPR) or top journals.

 - Excellent understanding of core machine learning concepts.

 - Excellent knowledge of the foundations of statistics, linear algebra, probability and machine learning.

 - Excellent skills in Python and PyTorch.

 - Experience in deep learning and computational pathology. Experience in self-supervised learning, survival analysis, multi-modal learning, domain adaptation, causal inference or model interpretability is a bonus.

source_jobs.title: Member of Technical Staff, Research Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ATARAXIS AI

Ataraxis is a clinical AI research lab working at the intersection of multi-modal AI and precision medicine. Our goal is to make disease predictable. To accomplish this, we develop new AI methods that predict patient outcomes and treatment response, and build clinical tools to assist physicians in selecting the most optimal treatments for their patients.

Our AI research lab discovers and develops methods to recognize patterns and predict outcomes across complex, multi-modal clinical data. This spans our causality https://www.ataraxis.ai/research-causality (Ataraxis™ Tau), foundation model https://www.ataraxis.ai/research---representation-learning (Falcon and Kestrel for digital pathology), and survival analysis https://www.ataraxis.ai/research-survival-analysis research.

Our first clinical products, such as Ataraxis™ Breast https://www.ataraxis.ai/ataraxis-breast-overview for breast cancer, already help patients get the most appropriate treatment across the best academic institutions and community clinics worldwide.

At Ataraxis, you will have a unique opportunity to shape not only the future of our company, but also the future of healthcare. You will join an exceptional team at the forefront of clinical AI research and deployment. Our advisors include AI pioneers such as our founding advisor, Yann LeCun, and distinguished oncologists from top cancer research institutions, all united by the mission to redefine precision medicine.

Ataraxis has raised over $24 million in funding, including a $20 million Series A led by top venture capital funds such as Thiel Capital/Founders Fund (OpenAI, SpaceX, Palantir), Obvious Ventures (AMI Labs, Inceptive, Radical Numerics, Recursion), and AIX Ventures (Hugging Face, Perplexity).

We are an company with a flat organizational structure, where every team member is empowered to actively contribute. Leadership roles are earned by those who demonstrate initiative and consistently deliver exceptional results. Strong work ethic and the ability to prioritize ruthlessly are essential.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Implement novel machine learning models and methods for self-supervised learning, survival analysis, multi-modal learning, causality and interpretability.

 - Translate machine learning and statistics papers into production-ready code.

 - Build robust model evaluation frameworks and monitor model performance.

 - Develop pipelines for data preprocessing, integration, and quality assurance.

 - Maintain high standards of scientific documentation to ensure reproducibility and clarity in both internal reports and external publications.

 - Optimize code to run efficiently on GPU clusters, with emphasis on speed and scalability.

 - Deploy machine learning models to the cloud in optimized inference pipelines.

 - Develop and maintain regression and unit tests to ensure high-quality code.

 - Disseminate the results by co-authoring research papers and abstracts.

 - Collaborate with a multidisciplinary team of engineers and scientists.
   
   


QUALIFICATIONS

 - BS/MS/PhD degree in computer science, machine learning or statistics.

 - Excellent understanding of core machine learning concepts.

 - Excellent knowledge of the foundations of statistics, linear algebra and probability.

 - Excellent skills in Python and PyTorch.

 - Proficiency in data visualization and communicating complex results to both technical and non-technical audiences.

 - Excellent understanding of computer architecture, parallel training of AI models, and GPU optimization.

 - Experience in deep learning. Experience in at least one of {self-supervised learning, survival analysis, multi-modal learning, domain adaptation, causal inference, model interpretability, computational pathology} is a plus but is not critical.

 - Attention to detail and ability to drive tasks to completion.

 - Passion for research. Prior publications in A* conferences (e.g. ICML, ICLR, NeurIPS, CVPR) are a plus but are not crit

source_jobs.title: Member of Technical Staff, Translational Science
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ATARAXIS AI

Ataraxis is a clinical AI research lab working at the intersection of multi-modal AI and precision medicine. Our goal is to make disease predictable. To accomplish this, we develop new AI methods that predict patient outcomes and treatment response, and build clinical tools to assist physicians in selecting the most optimal treatments for their patients.

Our AI research lab discovers and develops methods to recognize patterns and predict outcomes across complex, multi-modal clinical data. This spans our causality https://www.ataraxis.ai/research-causality (Ataraxis™ Tau), foundation model https://www.ataraxis.ai/research---representation-learning (Falcon and Kestrel for digital pathology), and survival analysis https://www.ataraxis.ai/research-survival-analysis research.

Our first clinical products, such as Ataraxis™ Breast https://www.ataraxis.ai/ataraxis-breast-overview for breast cancer, already help patients get the most appropriate treatment across the best academic institutions and community clinics worldwide.

At Ataraxis, you will have a unique opportunity to shape not only the future of our company, but also the future of healthcare. You will join an exceptional team at the forefront of clinical AI research and deployment. Our advisors include AI pioneers such as our founding advisor, Yann LeCun, and distinguished oncologists from top cancer research institutions, all united by the mission to redefine precision medicine.

Ataraxis has raised over $24 million in funding, including a $20 million Series A led by top venture capital funds such as Thiel Capital/Founders Fund (OpenAI, SpaceX, Palantir), Obvious Ventures (AMI Labs, Inceptive, Radical Numerics, Recursion), and AIX Ventures (Hugging Face, Perplexity).

We are an company with a flat organizational structure, where every team member is empowered to actively contribute. Leadership roles are earned by those who demonstrate initiative and consistently deliver exceptional results. Strong work ethic and the ability to prioritize ruthlessly are essential.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Generating scientific/clinical insights from data produced by the AI models. Identifying areas to improve for the AI models.

 - Collaborating on and contributing to publications in scientific and clinical journals.

 - Summarizing the research in the form of abstracts to be presented at scientific meetings.

 - Collaborating with a team of AI researchers, AI engineers, biostatisticians and translational scientists.

 - Collaborating with external clinical partners.

 - Drafting applications for access to clinical trial data.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - MD or PhD in a scientific discipline.

 - Passion for research, attention to detail and ability to drive tasks to completion.

 - Prior lead author papers published in high profile scientific and/or clinical journals.

 - Excellent understanding of basic machine learning concepts.

 - Excellent skills in Python.

 - Exceptional spoken and written communication skills.

 - Experience in cancer/oncology research is a bonus.

notes: Clinical AI research lab with strong focus on engineering and research roles
